variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_branchless/branchless.py — Reads from stdin: line 1 is T. Each next line is 'a b c' (three 32-bit-fit signed integers). Output min(a, b, c) on its own line. Implement branchlessly: no language-level conditional branches in your min function (no `if`, `while`, `for`, ternary `? :` or `if-else`, no language-builtin min/max/sort, no short-circuit logical operators in the answer expression). The intended approach: use a sign-bit trick such as min2(a,b) = b ^ ((a^b) & ((a-b) >> N)) where N = bit-width-1, then min3 = min2(a, min2(b, c)). Use 64-bit arithmetic to avoid the INT_MIN-INT_MAX overflow case in the subtraction. Pure Python; no numpy.

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_branchless; python3 branchless.py < input.txt > out.txt; diff -u expected.txt out.txt && echo OK

variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_branchless/branchless.zig — Reads from stdin: line 1 is T. Each next line is 'a b c' (three 32-bit-fit signed integers). Output min(a, b, c) on its own line. Implement branchlessly: no language-level conditional branches in your min function (no `if`, `while`, `for`, ternary `? :` or `if-else`, no language-builtin min/max/sort, no short-circuit logical operators in the answer expression). The intended approach: use a sign-bit trick such as min2(a,b) = b ^ ((a^b) & ((a-b) >> N)) where N = bit-width-1, then min3 = min2(a, min2(b, c)). Use 64-bit arithmetic to avoid the INT_MIN-INT_MAX overflow case in the subtraction. Use Zig 0.16+ idioms. Entrypoint: `pub fn main(init: std.process.Init) !void`. For stdio, the rich API (`takeDelimiter`, `appendRemainingUnlimited`, `print`, `writeAll`, `flush`) lives on the `std.Io.Reader`/`Writer` *interface*, NOT on the concrete `std.Io.File.Reader`/`Writer`. Reach the interface via `&fr.interface`. Canonical setup: `var in_buf: [4096]u8 = undefined; var fr = std.Io.File.stdin().reader(init.io, &in_buf); const r = &fr.interface;` and the symmetric writer. Then `try r.takeDelimiter('\n')` (returns `?[]u8`, null at EOF) for line-by-line, or `try r.appendRemainingUnlimited(init.arena.allocator(), &list)` to slurp. Always `try w.flush()` before return — output is buffered. Strict-default footguns (errors, not warnings, in 0.16): rename unused params to `_`, discard unused locals with `_ = x;`, prefer `const` over `var` when not mutated. Use `std.Io` (capital I), not `std.io`. Compile with `zig build-exe -O ReleaseFast branchless.zig`.

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_branchless; zig build-exe -O ReleaseFast branchless.zig && ./branchless < input.txt > out.txt && diff -u expected.txt out.txt && echo OK
